title: Lesson Quiz: Power, Authority, and Legitimacy
description: Assessment of the concept that power is the ability to control, authority is the right to use power, and legitimacy is public acceptance of that authority.

choices.text: The ability to influence or control people and decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. Power is the ability to influence or control.

choices.text: The public acceptance of a leader
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That describes legitimacy, not power.

choices.text: The right to use authority only during elections
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That does not define power.

choices.text: A list of citizen rights
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rights are not the same as power.

choices.text: The ability to ignore laws
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Authority is not ignoring laws.

choices.text: The recognized right to use power
choices.feedback: Correct. Authority is accepted as the right to use power.

choices.text: Public disagreement with leaders
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That does not define authority.

choices.text: The same thing as legitimacy
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Authority and legitimacy are related but not identical.

choices.text: The government has no power
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Legitimacy is not the absence of power.

choices.text: People believe the government’s authority is rightful
choices.feedback: Correct. Legitimacy means authority is accepted as justified.

choices.text: Citizens never question leaders
choices.feedback: Incorrect. People may still question leaders even in legitimate systems.

choices.text: Only one person makes all decisions
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That describes a power arrangement, not legitimacy.

choices.text: It makes rules less clear
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Legitimacy supports order and clarity, not confusion.

choices.text: It helps build trust and stability
choices.feedback: Correct. Public acceptance of authority helps create stability.

choices.text: It removes the need for laws
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Legitimacy does not remove the need for laws.

choices.text: It prevents any disagreement in society
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Disagreement can still happen even when legitimacy exists.

questions.prompt: What may happen if a government has power but lacks legitimacy?

choices.text: Citizens may resist or refuse to cooperate
choices.feedback: Correct. If authority is not accepted, resistance becomes more likely.

choices.text: Citizens automatically trust every decision
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Lack of legitimacy reduces trust.

choices.text: Authority becomes stronger because people disagree
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Disagreement does not automatically strengthen authority.

choices.text: Rules no longer matter in society
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rules may still exist even if legitimacy is weak.
